description:
You are a capacity-planner who has just received performance logs from three
    nodes of a small distributed system.  
    Your goal is to parse the raw CSV logs, calculate a few aggregate
    statistics for every node, decide whether each resource (CPU, memory,
    network) needs an UPGRADE, MONITOR or is OK, and then write the
    machine-readable results to one single JSON document.

    1. Input data that already exists in the container  
       • Directory: /home/user/cluster_logs  
       • Files inside that directory  
         – nodeA.log  
         – nodeB.log  
         – nodeC.log  

       Every *.log file is a UTF-8 text file that follows this exact CSV
       schema (one header line + 5 data lines):  
       timestamp,cpu_percent,mem_mb,net_in_mb,net_out_mb

       Example line (do **not** hard-code):  
       2024-05-01T00:00:00Z,45,1024,50,20

    2. What you have to compute for **each** node  
       • average_cpu_percent  : mean of the five cpu_percent values (float)  
       • peak_memory_mb       : maximum mem_mb value (integer)  
       • total_network_mb     : sum of every net_in_mb and net_out_mb value
                                (integer)  
       • recommendation       : a comma-separated decision string with
                                exactly three fields in this order:
                                CPU:<STATUS>,MEM:<STATUS>,NET:<STATUS>  
                                STATUS must be one of UPGRADE, MONITOR, OK
                                produced using these rules:
                                – CPU: UPGRADE  if average_cpu_percent  > 70  
                                         MONITOR if 60 ≤ average_cpu_percent ≤ 70  
                                         OK      otherwise  
                                – MEM: UPGRADE  if peak_memory_mb       > 2000  
                                         OK      otherwise  
                                – NET: UPGRADE  if total_network_mb     > 400  
                                         OK      otherwise

    3. Output you must create  
       • Directory: /home/user/output  
         (create it if it does not exist)  
       • File:      /home/user/output/capacity_report.json

       Strict JSON format that the automated checker will parse:

       {
         "nodeA": {
           "average_cpu_percent": <float>,
           "peak_memory_mb":      <integer>,
           "total_network_mb":    <integer>,
           "recommendation":      "<STRING>"
         },
         "nodeB": {
           ...
         },
         "nodeC": {
           ...
         }
       }

       Formatting requirements that **must** be met:  
       a. Keys must appear in the exact order shown above for every node.  
       b. The file must end with a single trailing newline.  
       c. Whitespace (spaces, newlines) elsewhere is free-form; numeric
          values can be written with either 0 or 1 digit after the decimal
          point (e.g. 65 or 65.0) as long as they are valid JSON numbers.  
       d. Use lowercase node names ("nodeA", "nodeB", "nodeC" exactly),
          do not invent others.  
       e. recommendation must match the comma-separated format exactly, with
          no added spaces except the single commas shown above.

    4. What the agent should show on the terminal  
       • Any intermediate shell output is allowed.  
       • As the final step, print the entire contents of
         /home/user/output/capacity_report.json to STDOUT so the user can
         see the resulting report.

    5. Tools and limits  
       • Work only with standard GNU/Linux CLI utilities already present in
         the container (bash, grep, awk, sed, cut, etc.).  
       • The agent does NOT have root; stay inside /home/user.  
       • Do not hard-code the expected numbers—derive them from the log
         files so that the solution remains valid if the input changes.

    6. Quality assurance log  
       If you wish, you may store any parsing debug information in
       /home/user/output/debug.log, but the automated checker will only look
       at capacity_report.json.

    When you are finished, /home/user/output/capacity_report.json should be
    present, syntactically correct JSON, containing the aggregates and
    recommendations derived from the data in /home/user/cluster_logs/*.log.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data the automated evaluation will use.
        The contents below must NOT be exposed to the agent.
    -->

    <!-- Pre-existing files and their exact contents -->
    <preexisting>
        <dir path="/home/user/cluster_logs">
            <file name="nodeA.log"><![CDATA[
timestamp,cpu_percent,mem_mb,net_in_mb,net_out_mb
2024-05-01T00:00:00Z,45,1024,50,20
2024-05-01T01:00:00Z,55,1100,60,25
2024-05-01T02:00:00Z,65,1150,70,30
2024-05-01T03:00:00Z,75,1200,80,35
2024-05-01T04:00:00Z,85,1250,90,40
]]></file>

            <file name="nodeB.log"><![CDATA[
timestamp,cpu_percent,mem_mb,net_in_mb,net_out_mb
2024-05-01T00:00:00Z,35,2048,20,10
2024-05-01T01:00:00Z,40,2100,25,12
2024-05-01T02:00:00Z,60,2200,35,15
2024-05-01T03:00:00Z,70,2300,45,18
2024-05-01T04:00:00Z,95,2400,55,20
]]></file>

            <file name="nodeC.log"><![CDATA[
timestamp,cpu_percent,mem_mb,net_in_mb,net_out_mb
2024-05-01T00:00:00Z,20,512,10,5
2024-05-01T01:00:00Z,25,520,12,6
2024-05-01T02:00:00Z,30,530,14,7
2024-05-01T03:00:00Z,40,540,16,8
2024-05-01T04:00:00Z,50,550,18,9
]]></file>
        </dir>
    </preexisting>

    <!-- Calculated aggregates that must appear in the JSON report -->
    <expected_json><![CDATA[
{
  "nodeA": {
    "average_cpu_percent": 65.0,
    "peak_memory_mb": 1250,
    "total_network_mb": 500,
    "recommendation": "CPU:MONITOR,MEM:OK,NET:UPGRADE"
  },
  "nodeB": {
    "average_cpu_percent": 60.0,
    "peak_memory_mb": 2400,
    "total_network_mb": 255,
    "recommendation": "CPU:MONITOR,MEM:UPGRADE,NET:OK"
  },
  "nodeC": {
    "average_cpu_percent": 33.0,
    "peak_memory_mb": 550,
    "total_network_mb": 105,
    "recommendation": "CPU:OK,MEM:OK,NET:OK"
  }
}
]]></expected_json>

    <!-- Where the agent must write the report -->
    <output path="/home/user/output/capacity_report.json"/>
